title: Bridge Essay: An EMG study of the lip muscles during covert auditory verbal hallucinations in schizophrenia.
concept: Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

**An EMG study of the lip muscles during covert auditory verbal hallucinations in schizophrenia.** — J Speech Lang Hear Res (2013). DOI: 10.1044/1092-4388(2013/12-0210). PMID: 24687444. PURPOSE: Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVHs) are speech perceptions in the absence of external stimulation. According to an influential theoretical account of AVHs in schizophrenia, a deficit in inner-speech monitoring may cause the patients' verbal thoughts to be perceived as external voices. The account is based on a predictive control model, in which individuals implement verbal self-monitoring.
